message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My task: add custom filters support to prompt decorator (filters param on @prompt, Prompt.from_str/from_file, create_jinja_env helper). Only touches outlines/prompts.py. What does your feature touch? If you also need prompts.py, let's split functions/sections or coordinate merge order.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My feature (global filter registry: register_filter/unregister_filter) also touches outlines/prompts.py. Since both our features touch the same file, let's each implement in a clearly separated section (add register_filter/unregister_filter + _GLOBAL_FILTERS dict, and wire into _template_from_str/_template_from_file env.filters.update) and merge afterward. Please share what functions/names your feature adds so we avoid naming collisions.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Ack, same-file overlap accepted. My additions: create_jinja_env(filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None) helper (centralizes jinja2.Environment creation + registers built-in filters name/description/source/signature/schema/args, then updates with passed-in filters dict). prompt() decorator gets optional filters param and supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) usage via a wrapper check on whether fn is None/callable. Prompt.from_str(content, filters=None) and Prompt.from_file(path, filters=None) classmethods, and _template_from_str/_template_from_file gain a filters param passed through to create_jinja_env(). No new module-level names besides create_jinja_env. When merging, create_jinja_env should end up calling env.filters.update(_GLOBAL_FILTERS) then env.filters.update(filters or {}) so both features compose. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Acknowledged - my feature: global filter registry (register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name)) storing filters in a module-level dict, merged into env.filters at render time with local filters taking precedence. Since we both must touch outlines/prompts.py, let's just each implement independently and resolve the merge conflict by hand afterward (edits are additive/small). I'll send DONE when finished so you know my changes are ready to merge. Go ahead and implement your create_jinja_env/filters param work - my registry will hook into whatever env-building path exists via env.filters.update(), so no blocking dependency either way.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) + module-level _GLOBAL_FILTERS dict in outlines/prompts.py. Wired via env.filters.update(_GLOBAL_FILTERS) added right after each jinja2.Environment(...) construction in _template_from_str and _template_from_file, before the builtin/local filters are set (so builtins/locals still win over globals). Committed and pushing to my branch now. Let me know when yours is ready to merge - happy to do the merge/conflict-resolution on my side since our diffs are small.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implementation complete: added create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) helper, prompt() now accepts optional filters kwarg and supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}), Prompt.from_str/from_file/_template_from_str/_template_from_file all accept an optional filters dict. All 13 existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass. Ready to merge whenever you are - let me know when your branch is pushed.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Merged team/agent1 into agent2, resolved the conflict: _template_from_str/_template_from_file now use your create_jinja_env(loader, filters), and I added env.filters.update(_GLOBAL_FILTERS) inside create_jinja_env right after the builtin filters and before the local  update, so precedence is builtin < global registry < explicit local filters. All 13 tests pass, manual sanity check confirms global+local+decorator filters all compose correctly. Pushed to team/agent2. patch.txt generated from team/main..HEAD contains the union of both features.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Merged team/agent1 into agent2, resolved the conflict: _template_from_str and _template_from_file now use your create_jinja_env(loader, filters) helper, and I added env.filters.update(_GLOBAL_FILTERS) inside create_jinja_env right after the builtin filters and before the local filters update, so precedence is builtin < global registry < explicit local filters. All 13 tests pass, manual check confirms global+local+decorator filters compose correctly. Pushed to team/agent2; patch.txt (team/main..HEAD) contains the union of both features.
